ddls:
CREATE TABLE "connections" (
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each connection */,
  "application_name" TEXT /* Name of the application using the connection */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the connection */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the connection (e.g., active, closed, error) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the connection was created */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last activity on the connection */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the client making the connection */,
  "database_name" TEXT /* Name of the database the connection is to */,
  "connection_type" TEXT /* Type of connection (e.g., TCP, SSL) */,
  "encrypt" INTEGER /* Whether the connection is encrypted */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique session identifier for the connection */,
  "idle_time" INTEGER /* Time in seconds the connection has been idle */,
  "last_query" TEXT /* The last query executed on the connection */,
  "query_duration" INTEGER /* Duration in milliseconds of the last query */,
  "active_transactions" INTEGER /* Number of active transactions on the connection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connections_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connections_application_name FOREIGN KEY ("application_name") REFERENCES applications ("application_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "connection_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated connection */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "message" TEXT /* Description of the connection event or error */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the log entry (e.g., INFO, WARN, ERROR) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the client at the time of the log entry */,
  "database_name" TEXT /* Name of the database involved in the log entry */,
  "query" TEXT /* The query that was executed or attempted */,
  "query_duration" INTEGER /* Duration in milliseconds of the query */,
  "transaction_id" TEXT /* ID of the transaction associated with the log entry */,
  "stack_trace" TEXT /* Stack trace if the log entry is an error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connection_logs_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "application_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the application */,
  "application_type" TEXT /* Type of application (e.g., web, mobile, batch) */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the application runs (e.g., development, production) */,
  "last_deployed" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last deployment */,
  "version" TEXT /* Current version of the application */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., active, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last activity by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., wait_timeout) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "setting_group" TEXT /* Group to which the setting belongs (e.g., connection, performance) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the setting was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the setting is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., connection_error, performance_issue) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* ID or name of the person who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on how the alert was resolved */,
  "alert_source" TEXT /* Source of the alert (e.g., application, database) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)
